title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After the gunshot, Howard Ricks intervenes to save Alastair Brent's life by disarming the shooter, Frank Carter. Jane Olivia explains that Ricks grabbed Carter and seized his gun. Despite Carter's protests that the entire situation is a setup, Ricks drags him away to be turned over to the police. While Lady Olivia tends to a shaken Brent, Howard Ricks challenges Hercule Poirot to demonstrate his detective skills, citing a previous incident where Poirot caught the wrong person during an attempt on the Prime Minister's life.

Poirot remains suspicious of the circumstances. Although he believes Carter is a rogue with a flimsy story about being a spy, he finds it highly unusual that Ricks was present at two consecutive shooting attempts, both of which resulted in missed shots. During dinner, Poirot observes Ricks, suspecting the man is "wearing a sheep's skin." 

Later that night, Ricks visits Poirot's room to explain his actions. Ricks admits that during the previous incident involving the Prime Minister, he had staged a capture to allow a boy named Ram Lal to escape. However, he insists that today was different and that Carter was truly the shooter. Ricks confesses a contradictory motive: while he believes Brent should be killed for the sake of human progress, he personally intervened because Brent is a kind gentleman. The following morning, during a village church service, Poirot experiences a sudden epiphany while listening to the choir sing about being delivered from evil and avoiding traps. He realizes that he has almost fallen into a carefully laid, intricately woven trap.
